name: No Filter
blacks: The real reason the project got delayed: ______.
Our CEO just announced that starting Monday, everyone must ______.
Studies show that 9 out of 10 employees prefer ______ over a pay rise.
The new company values are: integrity, innovation, and ______.
During the all-hands, someone accidentally shared their screen showing ______.
HR has issued a new policy strictly prohibiting ______ in the office.
The team-building activity nobody asked for: ______.
What's really happening in that “quick sync“ nobody put an agenda on? ______.
This quarter's biggest blocker wasn't the tech debt. It was ______.
We don't talk about the incident at the 2023 Christmas party. The incident was ______.
The real reason Dave from accounting got fired was ______.
My therapist says my biggest workplace trauma is ______.
The job posting said “fast-paced environment“. What they meant was ______.
I didn't get the promotion because of ______.
The company's new AI strategy is basically ______ with a GPT wrapper.
What do senior engineers actually do all day? ______.
The leadership retreat ended with everyone agreeing on ______.
My exit interview consisted entirely of me explaining ______.
The Glassdoor review that got the CEO fired mentioned ______.
I've been at this company for five years. My reward? ______.
According to the company handbook, consensual ______ is still prohibited.
The one thing nobody mentions in the diversity report: ______.
The investor pitch was going great until someone brought up ______.
Our “culture fit“ screening process is basically just ______.
The reason the last three CTOs quit was ______.
What's really in the executives' Slack DMs? ______.
The board approved the budget for ______ but not for raises.
At the start-up, we replaced the HR department with ______.
The onboarding buddy program has been replaced by ______.
The CEO's vision for 2025 is ______. Nobody knows what it means.
whites: A passive-aggressive Slack message.
Replying all to a company-wide email.
A 47-slide PowerPoint with no takeaways.
The intern who somehow got promoted.
An “urgent“ request sent at 5:58 PM on Friday.
A manager who says “let's take this offline“ every five minutes.
Death by a thousand meetings.
Pretending to take notes while online shopping.
The person who microwaves fish in the kitchen.
A LinkedIn post about “lessons learned“ after being laid off.
Turning your camera off and immediately going to make coffee.
A Jira ticket with no description, no assignee, and maximum priority.
The slide that says “synergies“.
Blocking two hours in someone's calendar with no context.
A team retrospective that changes absolutely nothing.
The guy who asks a question that's actually a comment.
An OKR that no one remembers by Q2.
Agile but make it chaotic.
A mandatory fun Friday.
The last person to speak before the meeting ends.
A “quick question“ that takes 45 minutes.
The roadmap that changes every sprint.
Saying “great question“ before having no answer.
A post-it wall that solves nothing.
Working on vacation “just for a bit“.
The design system that nobody follows.
A KPI that nobody actually tracks.
A stakeholder who shows up on the last day of the sprint.
Renaming the folder “FINAL_v3_ACTUALLYFINAL“.
The endless email thread that could have been a Slack message.
A Zoom link that doesn't work.
The quarterly review no one prepared for.
An offsite that's basically a normal workday but in a hotel.
Micromanaging via read receipts.
The team wiki nobody updates.
Moving fast and breaking everything.
A bug that's “not a bug, it's a feature“.
The two-hour onboarding that explains nothing.
A feedback form that gets ignored.
The coworker who volunteers you for extra work.
A rebranding nobody asked for.
Promising a delivery date you both know is impossible.
The shared Google Doc with 14 conflicting versions.
A pivot that undoes six months of work.
Turning a 10-minute fix into a three-sprint epic.
The Slack channel that's basically a second inbox.
A motivational quote in the company newsletter.
The team photo where nobody looks happy.
A performance review based on vibes.
Cancelling the meeting the morning it was supposed to happen.
Crying in the toilet between calls.
A situationship with the team lead.
Sending a resignation letter and immediately regretting it.
A very suspicious “working from home“ arrangement.
Blaming the junior dev for a decision made in 2019.
Getting drunk at the Christmas party and being honest for once.
Discovering your salary on Glassdoor and having an existential crisis.
A passive income side hustle done entirely on company time.
An affair started during a two-day offsite in Seville.
Lying on your CV about knowing Excel.
The CTO who hasn't written code since 2008.
Quietly quitting but still showing up to every meeting.
A performance improvement plan that's just a polite firing.
Faking a bad connection to get out of a call.
The entire engineering team being replaced by one prompt.
A NDА nobody read but everyone signed.
An all-expenses-paid business trip that's mostly a bender.
A 360 review written by your worst enemy.
Billing “research“ hours for watching YouTube.
The reason two colleagues stopped making eye contact at standup.
Sending “accidentally“ a spicy message to the work group chat.
Getting caught badmouthing the boss on Teams.
A severance package that barely covers therapy.
An equity stake that's worth nothing if you leave before 2031.
Finding out your job was posted on LinkedIn while you still work there.
The slide deck that got someone put on gardening leave.
A wellness benefit that's just a Headspace subscription.
Using “personal reasons“ to mean “I hate it here“.
A colleague who CC's your manager on everything.
The rumour that HR is sleeping with the CFO.
Accidentally liking a photo from 2015 on the CEO's Instagram.
A “no meetings Wednesday“ that immediately fills with meetings.
The promotion that came with a title change and no money.
A culture committee that exists to manage complaints about culture.
Stealing someone else's idea in a meeting and saying it louder.
A DEI initiative that consists of one poster in the kitchen.
Getting caught playing Wordle during a strategy session.
An anonymous survey that's clearly not anonymous.
Oversharing at the team lunch after two glasses of wine.
A Notion page with 200 unfinished documents.
The company chatbot that hallucinates the company's own policies.
A hackathon prize that's a gift voucher for the company's own product.
Printing your resignation letter on the office printer.
A LinkedIn endorsement for “Microsoft Word“ from your CEO.
The Friday email that starts with “Quick one before the weekend“.
A colleague who always schedules meetings at 8 AM.
Being voluntold to lead the onboarding for someone more senior than you.
A zero-hours contract with a full-time workload.
The manager who took credit for your work at the conference.
An “unlimited holiday“ policy where nobody takes holidays.
A team dinner where the bill gets split equally and you only had a salad.
Realising the company values are just vibes.
